mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved profit expectations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 2.0% signals tightening monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high FEDFUNDS regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.05 in tightening environments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal stagflation risks, compressing earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in co-occurring CPI/FEDFUNDS spikes exceed 1.2% or IC < -0.02 in overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL > 270) during expansionary cycles signals central bank intervention, creating volatility that reduces S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 270 periods exceed 3.0% or IC > 0.04 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.03 in labor expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines below 5.0% signal tight labor market pressures, increasing wage inflation and reducing corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in UNRATE < 5.0% periods exceed 1.0% or IC > -0.02 in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days through improved economic outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1/HOUST growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.03 in demand expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) indicates strong consumer demand and accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in RSAFS/FEDFUNDS co-occurrence periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.02 in low-rate demand environments.
